title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with eye infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacteria that can cause conjunctivitis. The active ingredient, echinacea, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote the healing process by stimulating the immune system. By taking herbal Kansas coneflower capsules regularly, individuals can support their body's natural defenses against eye infections, reducing symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidants can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye infection and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with eye infections such as conjunctivitis (pink eye) or uveitis, by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Immune-stimulating properties: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulatory effects can help boost the body's natural defense against infection, promoting a healthy immune response to combat eye infections and supporting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with eye infections, such as conjunctivitis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids: These compounds may exhibit antioxidant properties, which could help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially contributing to the prevention or treatment of eye infections.

Alkaloids (e.g., echinacoside, isobutylamides): These compounds may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help combat bacterial or viral infections in the eyes and reduce associated inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part for eye infections due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are another commonly used part, as they contain a significant amount of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: Flowers are also used, particularly for their high content of essential oils, including echinacoside, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia at the end of the growing season.
Clean the roots thoroughly with a fine mesh sieve to remove any impurities.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a filler material like cellulose in a ratio.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them to complete the capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with eye infection because they contain calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces redness and swelling in the eyes. The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold also help to combat bacterial and fungal infections, promoting healing and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, calendula's soothing properties ease pain and itchiness, providing relief from conjunctivitis and other eye irritations. As a result, pot marigold capsules offer a natural and effective solution for treating eye infections and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of Calendula capsules inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause eye infections, preventing further infection and promoting recovery.
Immune-boosting: Calendula's immune-boosting properties stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, helping to fight off pathogens and promote a faster recovery from eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection in the eyes.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which aid in protecting the eyes from oxidative damage and preventing the spread of infection.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in soothing and healing the infected eye tissue, reducing swelling and promoting the recovery process.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part due to their high content of flavonoids, carotenoids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their rich content of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
Seeds: The seeds are occasionally used, but less commonly than flowers and leaves, due to their lower content of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Dry the calendula flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the calendula powder with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the calendula mixture using a powder funnel and capsule filling device.
Store the calendula capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with eye infection because they contain the potent herb Euphrasia officinalis, which has been used for centuries to treat eye ailments. The herb's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discharge. Additionally, eyebright's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, allowing for faster healing and relief from discomfort. By targeting the root cause of the infection, eyebright capsules provide effective natural relief from eye infections such as pink eye and conjunctivitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright's antimicrobial properties combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause eye infections, preventing their growth and spread.
Antioxidant: Eyebright's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of chronic eye diseases.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing the eye infection.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and recovery from eye infection.

Alkaloids (e.g. euphrasin, rosmarinic acid): These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to combat the underlying infection and reduce inflammation in the eyes, promoting recovery and relief from eye infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: They are used because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids and glycosides, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
Stems: They are used as they contain bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and fight infections in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of the aerial parts of euphrasia officinalis at the peak of flowering stage in early summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm well-ventilated area at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle with 1 gram of silica.
Mix the plant powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of glycerin in a double boiler at 80°c.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 0.25 grams of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with eye infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsule's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. This helps alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, such as redness, itchiness, and swelling. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Dandelion's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections in the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion capsules reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with eye infections.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate infection, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye infections.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly inulin, in Taraxacum officinale capsules exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation in the eyes, helping to combat eye infections.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin in Taraxacum officinale capsules, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and microbial growth in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in soothing and treating eye infections.
Roots: They are used to make capsules because they contain taraxasterol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in treating eye infections.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing swelling and treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried root and leaves of taraxacum officinale from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 50g of the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the powder with 100g of a natural binding agent such as gelatin to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the taraxacum officinale powder mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye infection because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsule's active ingredients, specifically flavonoids and terpenes, work together to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause conjunctivitis. Additionally, the antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the eyes, promoting a speedy recovery from infection. As a result, Maidenhair tree capsules offer a natural and effective solution for relieving the symptoms of eye infections, allowing patients to recover quickly and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: The capsules contain a compound called Podophyllotoxin, which has antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause conjunctivitis and other eye infections.

Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, relieving symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge associated with eye infections.

Antioxidant property: The capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and other eye diseases.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye infections such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Terpenoids like bilobalide have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing the severity of eye infections.
Bilobin (a type of flavonoid): Bilobin's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of eye infections and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used for medicinal purposes due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Seeds: The seeds are also used, but with caution, as they contain a toxic compound called bilobalide.
Barks: The barks are used, although less commonly, in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of ginkgo biloba leaves in the autumn season when they are fully ripe.
Clean and dry the ginkgo biloba leaves for 2-3 hours to prevent contamination.
Crush 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 10g of gelatin and 10ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 50-100 empty gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the ginkgo biloba mixture each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eye infection because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe red, itchy eyes and reduce swelling. The aloe vera gel in the capsules penetrates deep into the eye tissues to combat bacterial and viral infections, promoting quick recovery. Additionally, its antimicrobial agents prevent further infection and promote healthy tear production, reducing discomfort and restoring clear vision. By targeting the root cause of the infection, herbal aloe capsules provide effective relief from conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, making them effective against eye infections like conjunctivitis.

Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera contains antibacterial compounds like lupeol and salannin, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye or other eye infections by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from spreading.

Wound-healing properties: Herbal aloe capsules promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the production of antioxidants, which helps speed up the recovery process for infected eyes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone glycoside that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria causing eye infections.
Aloin: Aloin is an anthraquinone glycoside that possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe and protect the eyes from infection.

Saponins: Saponins are a group of triterpenoid glycosides that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms causing eye infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is used to create capsules for eye infections due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: This gel is rich in vitamins and minerals, which helps in reducing swelling and promoting healing in eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from mature leaves by cutting off yellow tips and rinsing with cold water.
Dehydrate the aloe vera gel using a food dehydrator at 135 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Mix the dehydrated aloe vera gel with gelatin powder in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe vera gel mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with eye infection because they possess potent antioxidant properties that effectively combat oxidative stress in the eyes. The herbs present in these capsules, such as Bacopa monnieri, have been traditionally used to treat various ocular disorders, including conjunctivitis and blepharitis. The anti-inflammatory effects of Brahmi capsules help reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with eye infections, while its antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi in the eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a type of triterpenoid saponin that help reduce inflammation in the eyes and promote healing in cases of eye infections.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Bacopa monnieri exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation associated with eye infections.

Alkaloids (specifically, brahmine): Brahmine alkaloids in Bacopa monnieri have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause eye infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bacosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of eye infections.
Roots: They contain bacosides and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 500 grams of bacopa monnieri powder to be used for making herbal capsules.
Mix 250 grams of gelatin powder with 250 grams of glycerin and 50 grams of water in a bowl.
Add 250 grams of bacopa monnieri powder to the gelatin mixture and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Fill 1000 count empty gelatin capsules with the bacopa monnieri mixture and seal the capsules.
Store the bacopa monnieri capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower's antimicrobial compounds, such as cinnamic acid and chlorogenic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause eye infections, helping to alleviate symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in purple coneflower, including quercetin and kaempferol, reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye infections, promoting healing and comfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with eye infection because they contain antibacterial properties that target the root cause of the infection. The herbal extract, also known as Echinacea purpurea, has been traditionally used to combat infections and inflammation in the eyes. It works by reducing redness, swelling, and discharge, promoting healing and alleviating discomfort. By taking purple coneflower capsules regularly, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms and shorten the duration of an eye infection, making it a natural and effective solution for maintaining healthy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Icariin, a flavonoid glycoside, may help with eye infection by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness associated with eye infections.
Cichoric Acid: Cichoric acid, a sesquiterpene lactone, has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause eye infections.

remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The primary part of Echinacea purpurea used for medicinal purposes due to their high concentration of active compounds.
Roots: Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes are also used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Roots: Since the roots are the most commonly used part of Echinacea purpurea, I've added a third item, but if you would like a different part,  I could use Flowers: Flowers: The flowers are used for their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties to help with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of echinacea purpurea roots and rhizomes in late summer or early fall.
Clean the harvested roots and rhizomes with a mixture of 10% vinegar and 90% water for 30 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots and rhizomes in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 200 mesh.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400-500 milligrams of the ground echinacea purpurea powder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with eye infection because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains bioactive compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to possess antibacterial and antiviral activities. When taken in capsule form, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and can reach the eyes, where they help to combat infections and reduce inflammation. This natural remedy can provide relief from symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge, promoting a faster recovery from eye infections.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that cause eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections like blepharitis or pink eye.

Naphthodianthrones: These compounds have been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal activities, making them effective against a wide range of microorganisms that can cause eye infections, including fungi and bacteria.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phloroglucinol derivative that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat eye infections by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of pathogens.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by eye infections, promoting healing and reducing symptoms.

Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These naphthodianthrones have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which help combat eye infections by inhibiting the replication of viruses and bacteria that cause eye infections, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used because they also contain hypericin and hyperforin, which can help reduce inflammation and combat infection.
Stems: They are used as they contain a smaller amount of hypericin and hyperforin, but still possess some anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers from a clean and sanitized area with gloves.
Crush the dried flowers using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder into a fine powder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 5 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the gelatin mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with eye infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains various compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, which are common symptoms of eye infections such as conjunctivitis and pink eye. By reducing these symptoms, stinging nettle capsules can help alleviate discomfort, pain, and redness associated with eye infections, allowing for faster recovery and improved vision. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in treating eye infections.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica contain bioactive compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating eye infections.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain similar compounds to the leaves and roots, providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can aid in treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Clean and dry the harvested urtica dioica material by spreading it out in a single layer for 24 hours.
Grind 50 grams of the dried urtica dioica material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground urtica dioica powder with 150 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Encapsulate the urtica dioica gelatin mixture using a capsule machine with a 500 mg capsule size.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with eye infection because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The root extract in valerian capsules has been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with conjunctivitis, a common cause of eye infections. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part valeriana officinalis root powder with two parts vegetable glycerin in a small bowl to create a potent extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-300mg of the prepared valeriana officinalis extract and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

Roots: Contain azulene, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers measuring 1000mg per capsule.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered chamomile flowers with gelatin or vegetable capsules in a ratio of 1:4 for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes until it reaches desired consistency.
Mix the powdered herb with 50 grams of silica gel in a bowl to create an infusion blend for 10 minutes.
Transfer the infusion blend to a coffee grinder and grind it for 2 minutes to create a uniform mixture for capsule filling.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the uniform mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark capsules helps with eye infection because of its antibacterial properties, which effectively combat bacterial infections that cause conjunctivitis. Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Jesuit's Bark possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with eye infections such as redness, itchiness, and swelling.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality cinchona officinalis leaves ensuring their freshness for future use.
Grind dried cinchona officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 250 milligrams of powdered cinchona officinalis with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan stirring constantly over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain through a cheesecloth filter into a clean glass jar discarding solids.
Fill empty capsules with the resulting herbal infusion and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with eye infection because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages in these capsules, such as althaea officinalis, create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Marshmallow root has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause eye infections, including Staphylococcus aureus and Candida albicans.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the eyes from inflammation.
Seeds: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce eye redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis dried to a moisture content of 10% within 24 hours.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered marshmallow root with 500mg of cornstarch in a ratio of 1:1 and sift through a 60-mesh sieve for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture weighing approximately 400mg per capsule and seal immediately to preserve potency within 2 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature protected from light and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with eye infection because it is rich in allantoin, a natural compound that promotes tissue repair and regeneration. When taken orally as a capsule, comfrey's healing properties can be absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the eyes, where they help to soothe and calm infected tissues. The anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of comfrey also aid in reducing redness, swelling, and discharge associated with eye infections, promoting a speedy recovery and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Allantoin content: Allantoin, a key compound found in Comfrey, has anti-inflammatory and tissue-healing properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye infections.

Wound healing activity: Comfrey's high concentration of mucilages and polysaccharides may aid in the repair and regeneration of damaged corneal tissue, promoting faster recovery from eye infections and reducing scarring.

Antioxidant properties: Comfrey's antioxidant compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, can help protect the eyes against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development or progression of eye infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat inflammation and reduce swelling, making them effective for treating eye infections.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate redness and discomfort associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade or air circulation for 7-10 days to prevent spoilage and contamination.
Crush the dried symphytum officinale leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder taking care not to overprocess.
Mix 500mg of the powdered symphytum officinale with 2500mg of natural filler material such as potato starch or silica gel for easy capsule filling.
Fill #00 size empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal immediately storing in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise capsules helps with eye infection because they contain the essential oil of Pimpinella anisum, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the eyes, promoting a faster recovery from conjunctivitis or other eye infections. Additionally, the anise oil's antioxidant properties may also help protect the eyes from further damage and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Anise capsules contain compounds like anethole, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against various microorganisms, including those responsible for eye infections.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids present in anise capsules, such as apiin and apigenin, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, making them a natural remedy for red, itchy, or inflamed eyes.

Antioxidant property: Anise capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to age-related macular degeneration, cataracts, and other eye problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, roots are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory gel that helps to alleviate symptoms of eye infections.
Seeds: The seeds contain compounds with antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing bacterial and fungal growth associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried pimpinella anisum powder and 1000ml of boiling water for the decoction.
Steep the pimpinella anisum powder in the boiling water for 30 minutes to create a potent infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Mix 250g of glycerin and 50ml of distilled water with the strained infusion and stir well to create a concentrated liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 teaspoon of the pimpinella anisum liquid extract and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with eye infection because they contain flavonoids and antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge. The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel also aid in combating bacterial and viral infections that cause conjunctivitis. Additionally, the expectorant properties of fennel capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the eyes, promoting natural healing and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate eye infection symptoms without resorting to harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Fennel capsules contain compounds like limonene, anethole, and saponins that have antibacterial activity, which helps to inhibit the growth of bacteria causing eye infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in fennel have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye infections.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, which helps to protect against further inflammation and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing phytonutrients that have antibacterial properties, useful in combating bacterial infections causing conjunctivitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds in their optimal ripeness for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested seeds thoroughly using low-temperature air circulation or desiccant for 24 hours.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered seeds with 500mg of vegetable cellulose capsules and a small amount of glycerin according to the manufacturer's instructions for 10 minutes.
Fill and seal the capsules immediately and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with eye infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacteria and viruses that cause eye infections. The herbs' anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with conjunctivitis and other eye conditions. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, may help to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria that can cause eye infections, such as Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, which can help to combat the underlying infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help soothe and reduce inflammation associated with eye infections.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like camphor and borneol, rosemary rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat eye infections and improve ocular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source and dry them completely using air circulation for at least 7 days.
Grind the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Sift the powdered rosmarinus officinalis through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles for 2 minutes.
Mix the sifted powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler to create a decoction that is left to simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction and fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250 milligrams of the resulting liquid extract, which can be stored in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with eye infection because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause eye infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, thyme's antiseptic properties help to reduce discharge and alleviate redness, allowing for faster recovery from conjunctivitis and other eye infections. As a natural and non-invasive solution, herbal thyme capsules offer a safe and effective way to combat eye infections.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacterial eye infections by inhibiting the growth and replication of bacteria.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant Properties: Thyme's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.
Buds: Thyme buds have antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections causing conjunctivitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2000 milligrams of the powdered herb with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids after 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the strained infusion and seal them tightly before storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye infection are natural supplements containing a blend of herbs that help alleviate symptoms and promote healing of eye infections, such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis. The best herbal capsules for eye infection include Euphrasia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea angustifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Hydrastis canadensis, Aloe vera, Ulmus rubra, and Echinacea pallida. If left untreated, eye infections can cause blurred vision, eye redness, and discomfort, significantly impacting daily activities, work, and social interactions, ultimately affecting a person's quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye infections that are treated with herbal capsules are ear infections, sinusitis, and respiratory issues. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Garlic have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections. Additionally, herbs like Ginger and Turmeric provide natural relief from pain and inflammation associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye infection is Echinacea and Goldenseal. Echinacea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system, while Goldenseal's berberine content has antimicrobial and antibacterial properties that help combat infections. Combining these two herbs in capsule form can provide a powerful defense against eye infections and promote overall eye health. Some users also add Eyebright to the combination for its soothing and decongestant properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis capsules helps with eye infection because they contain a concentrated extract of the herb Eyebright, which has been traditionally used to treat eye problems for centuries. The active compounds in Euphrasia officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and fighting off infections in the eyes. By promoting healthy tissue and reducing inflammation, Euphrasia officinalis capsules can help alleviate symptoms of eye infections such as redness, discharge, and itching, providing relief and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye infections.
Phenolic Acids: They have antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause eye infections.
Triterpenoids: These compounds possess antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress during an eye infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and treat eye infections.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in treating eye infections and promoting eye health.
Stems: They contain bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of infection in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves from a clean and sterile environment.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place at 40-50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 2 grams of vegetable cellulose and add 10ml of water.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with eye infection because they contain immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, enhancing its ability to fight off invading pathogens. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea may help to reduce eye inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with eye infections. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea may also help to boost the immune system, supporting its ability to recover from an eye infection. This makes Echinacea purpurea a potentially beneficial supplement for supporting eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and fight off pathogens responsible for eye infections by exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
Caffeic acid phenethyl ester (CAPE): CAPE has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye infection symptoms and promote healing.
Isobutyl amide: This compound exhibits immunomodulatory effects, stimulating the body's immune response to fight off eye infections and prevent further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their content of caffeic acid derivatives, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat eye infections.
Flowers: The flowers are utilized for their high content of polysaccharides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects, which aid in treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried echinacea purpurea root and flower parts in late summer or early fall.
Clean the harvested material by gently rinsing it with water and pat drying it with paper towels.
Use a coffee grinder to finely grind 20 grams of the dried plant material into a powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the powdered echinacea purpurea mixture per capsule.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container to maintain their potency for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis capsules helps with eye infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work together to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, while also fighting off bacterial and fungal infections that can cause eye infections. Additionally, Calendula officinalis has natural antiseptic and antiviral properties that help to soothe and heal the eyes, promoting a faster recovery from eye infections. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat eye infections with great success.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections.
Flavonoids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial activity, which helps combat the underlying infection causing eye problems.
Carotenoids: They possess antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and heal eye infections.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide a similar set of benefits as the flowers, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in reducing eye infection symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula flowers at the end of the blooming season after 3 months of drying.
Mix the dried flowers with 100g of gelatin powder in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Add 20ml of glycerin to the mixture and stir well to create a homogenous paste in 10 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the paste and use it to fill 100 capsules in 30 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It improves blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in ginkgo biloba capsules also have antimicrobial properties, which help fight off the underlying infection. Additionally, ginkgo biloba capsules may reduce oxidative stress and improve the overall health of the eyes, making it a potential natural remedy for eye infections such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis. Regular use may also enhance vision clarity and reduce eye fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, which can aid in the healing of eye infections.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): This terpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress and promote recovery from eye infections.

Proanthocyanidins (specifically Ginkgolides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes, making them less susceptible to infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve eye health.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are used to make capsules for eye infections because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes.
Leaves (again): The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are also used due to their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of ginkgo biloba leaves and 20g of ginkgo biloba seeds from mature trees.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves and seeds in a well-ventilated area for 3 days.
Grind 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 10g of dried ginkgo biloba seeds into a fine powder.
Mix 1g of ground ginkgo biloba powder with 0.5g of starch and 0.5g of gelatin to form a mixture.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia capsules helps with eye infection because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system and promote natural healing. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea angustifolia can help reduce swelling and combat bacterial or viral infections in the eyes. This herb also has antiviral properties that can help prevent the replication of viruses, reducing the severity and duration of eye infections. Its natural properties can aid in soothing and healing the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, which can contribute to eye infections.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Echinacea angustifolia possess antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in this herb exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkylamides and polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system and have antiviral properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and mix with 1g of silica gel to prevent caking.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the chopped root mixture each.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with eye infection because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The gel from the Aloe vera plant, which is extracted and encapsulated, is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that promote healing and reduce inflammation. This natural remedy helps to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge, allowing the eye to recover faster and reducing the risk of further complications. It promotes a healthy environment for the eye to heal.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: Help soothe and reduce inflammation in the eyes, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
Anthraquinones: Exert antimicrobial properties, effectively reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause eye infections.
Polysaccharides: Stimulate the production of antibodies and activate the immune system, helping the body fight off eye infections more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
Gel from the Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is used because it has a high water content and is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to calm and protect the eye tissues.

Aloe Vera Extract from the Leaves: The aloe vera extract from the leaves is used because it contains a complex mixture of compounds, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that help to combat eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 pounds of aloe barbadensis gel from mature plants to ensure optimal quality.
Clean and dry the gel thoroughly using a sterile environment and a food dehydrator at 135°f for 24 hours.
Weigh and mix 250 grams of gel with 125 grams of  gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Mix the gelatin-gel mixture with 250 ml of distilled water while stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and fill 500-750 capsules within 10-15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis capsules helps with eye infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains berberine, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause eye infections. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of berberine help reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with eye infections. By addressing the underlying causes of the infection, Hydrastis canadensis capsules promote healing and soothe irritated eyes, ultimately leading to a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the eyes, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
Hydrastine: Hydrastine has strong anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help alleviate symptoms of eye infections such as redness, swelling, and discharge.
Vasaka (Vasakine): Vasakine has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of eye infections, promote healing, and prevent further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis are commonly used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Hydrastis canadensis are used because they contain berberine and hydrastine, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help treat eye infections.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Hydrastis canadensis are utilized for their berberine content, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root powder and sift it through a fine mesh to remove lumps.
Mix 50 grams of the sifted powder with 20 grams of cellulose powder to create a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with the blend at a dose of 250 milligrams per capsule.
Place the filled capsules in a paper bag and seal the opening with a twist tie to preserve freshness.
Label the bag with the date and contents and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other minerals and amino acids. These nutrients help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, promoting healing and comfort. Additionally, Aloe vera has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial and fungal infections, making it a natural and effective remedy for eye infections. This can aid in quick recovery.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat eye infections by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria.
Saponins: Saponins in Aloe vera have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and heal eye infections by reducing inflammation and preventing infection.

Polysaccharides (mainly Acemannan): Polysaccharides, particularly Acemannan, have potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help the body fight eye infections by reducing inflammation, enhancing immune response, and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a high concentration of aloe vera's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help treat eye infections.
Gel (from Leaves): The clear gel from the leaves is used because it has soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes.

Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): The extract from the leaves is used because it contains a concentrated form of aloe vera's active compounds, which can help to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried aloe vera powder in a coffee grinder for 2 minutes until fine powder forms.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the aloe vera powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of water to the aloe vera and gelatin mixture and stir until dissolved.
Fill gelatin capsules with the aloe vera mixture using a capsule filler for 10 capsules.
Seal the gelatin capsules using a capsule sealer and store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra capsules helps with eye infection because they contain the medicinal properties of the slippery elm tree, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in Ulmus rubra capsules may help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, while also promoting the healing of the conjunctiva. Additionally, the capsules' ability to protect and shield the eyes from environmental stressors may aid in the prevention of further infection and promote a healthy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins have astringent and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of microorganisms that cause eye infections.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation caused by eye infections.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat eye infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are commonly used to make capsules for eye infections due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infection.
Buds: The buds of Ulmus rubra are sometimes used to make capsules for eye infections due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ulmus rubra bark at 4pm after 3 days of partial sunlight.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at 40°c for 3 hours in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 200g of gelatin and 100ml of distilled water to make a gel-like substance.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida capsules helps with eye infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress makes it an effective natural remedy for combating eye infections. Its active compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, help to fight off pathogens and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, Echinacea pallida's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with eye infections.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the eye infection.
Caffeoylquinic acids: Caffeoylquinic acids have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress, a common consequence of eye infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea pallida are used to make capsules for eye infection due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea pallida are used to make capsules for eye infection due to their content of caffeic acid derivatives, which have antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea pallida are used to make capsules for eye infection due to their high concentration of polysaccharides, which have immunomodulatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried root and aerial parts of echinacea pallida under the right conditions for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested material in a low-temperature dryer at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Grind 2 parts of the dried root and aerial parts into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine with a 500-count capsule size.
